title: 7 Ways Data Centers Affect U.S. Communities
summary: A plain-English overview of how data centers can affect energy demand, water use, air quality, land use, equity, and local governance.
key_takeaway: Communities need transparency, enforceable standards, and site-specific impact review.

title: Data Centers in Virginia
source_organization: Virginia Joint Legislative Audit and Review Commission
summary: A 2024 legislative study of Virginia's data center industry covering electricity demand, water use, infrastructure costs, and tax incentives.
key_takeaway: Unconstrained data center growth produces large grid, water, and ratepayer impacts that local jurisdictions are often not staffed to review.

title: U.S. Data Center Energy Usage Report
summary: A federally funded national accounting of how much electricity U.S. data centers use today and how much they are projected to use through 2028.
key_takeaway: Data center electricity demand is rising faster than at any point in the last two decades, driven by AI workloads.
